debug_info.goal:
R : Type u_1
S : Type u_2
inst✝² : CommRing R
inst✝¹ : CommRing S
T : Type u_3
inst✝ : CommRing T
g : S ≃+* T
f : R ≃+* S
⊢ LeftInverse (fun x => f.symm (g.symm x)) fun x => g (f x)

intro x

      (fun x : R => f.symm (g.symm x)) (g (f x)) = f.symm (g.symm (g (f x))) := rfl

        rw [h]
      _ = x := by
        have h : f.symm (f x) = x := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [← f.symm_apply_apply x]
          <;> simp [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]

intro x
    calc
      f.symm (g.symm (g (f x))) = f.symm (f x) := by
        -- Use the property that g.symm and g are inverses
        have h : g.symm (g (f x)) = f x := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [RingEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
          <;> simp [f]
        rw [h]
      _ = x := by
        -- Use the property that f.symm and f are inverses
        have h : f.symm (f x) = x := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [RingEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
          <;> simp [f]
        rw [h]
  
  right_inv := by
    intro x
    calc
      g (f (f.symm (g.symm x))) = g (g.symm x) := by
        -- Use the property that f and f.symm are inverses
        have h : f (f.symm (g.symm x)) = g.symm x := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [RingEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
          <;> simp [f]
        rw [h]
      _ = x := by
        -- Use the property that g and g.symm are inverses
        have h : g (g.symm x) = x := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [RingEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
          <;> simp [g]
        rw [h]
  
  map_add' := by
    intro x y
    calc
      g (f (x + y)) = g (f x + f y) := by
        -- Use the property that f is a ring homomorphism
        have h : f (x + y) = f x + f y := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [RingEquiv.map_add]
          <;> simp [f]
        rw [h]
      _ = g (f x) + g (f y) := by
        -- Use the property that g is a ring homomorphism
        have h : g (f x + f y) = g (f x) + g (f y) := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [RingEquiv.map_add]
          <;> simp [g]
        rw [h]
      _ = g (f x) + g (f y) := by rfl
  
  map_mul' := by
    intro x y
    calc
      g (f (x * y)) = g (f x * f y) := by
        -- Use the property that f is a ring homomorphism
        have h : f (x * y) = f x * f y := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [RingEquiv.map_mul]
          <;> simp [f]
        rw [h]
      _ = g (f x) * g (f y) := by
        -- Use the property that g is a ring homomorphism
        have h : g (f x * f y) = g (f x) * g (f y) := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [RingEquiv.map_mul]
          <;> simp [g]
        rw [h]
      _ = g (f x) * g (f y) := by rfl
  
  map_one' := by
    calc
      g (f 1) = g 1 := by
        -- Use the property that f is a ring homomorphism
        have h : f 1 = 1 := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [RingEquiv.map_one]
          <;> simp [f]
        rw [h]
      _ = 1 := by
        -- Use the property that g is a ring homomorphism
        have h : g 1 = 1 := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          rw [RingEquiv.map_one]
          <;> simp [g]
        rw [h]

      have h2 : f.symm (f x) = x := by

    intro x
    have h3 : f.symm (g.symm (g (f x))) = x := h_main x
    exact h3

    have h₁ : f.symm (f x) = x := by
      apply Function.leftInverse_invFun
      exact f.symm
      <;> simp [Equiv.bijective]
    have h₂ : g.symm (g (f x)) = f x := by
      apply Function.leftInverse_invFun

    simp [h₁, h₂]
